parentQuestion: We can touch our finger to our nose while our eyes are closed because we can sen...

scaffolds.q: You can sense where your arm is even without looking at it.
scaffolds.explain: This sense of body position is called proprioception. Your brain constantly receives information about the position and movement of your body parts — a silent GPS system you never think about until it fails.

scaffolds.q: Muscle spindles detect the stretch of muscle fibers.
scaffolds.explain: Muscle spindles are proprioceptors located WITHIN muscles. They detect how much a muscle is stretched, helping the brain know the position of body parts. Stretch a muscle, stretch the spindle, fire the signal. Sit with this knowledge:drawn into the elegance of this transduction.
